title: gastrolobium dilatatum
intro: Gastrolobium dilatatum, also known as the poison flower, is a plant species native to Western Australia, known for its toxic and attractive features. The plant contains toxic compounds such as glycosides and alkaloids, which are traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties. The health benefits of Gastrolobium dilatatum include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, although it should be used with caution due to its toxicity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions or decoctions, often in combination with other herbs, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it.
